methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $5.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $2.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Helion is a Series F climate and energy company, currently valued at $5.4B post-money following $1B+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of nine comparable companies—one public (QuantumScape, ticker LNZA) and eight private firms in the same sector and valuation tier, including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and Form Energy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, which applies a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier from Series F to the next financing stage, implies a valuation of $7.0B. Peer-median analysis, based on the median valuation across seven same-sector comparables with similar capitalization, yields $5.0B. The peer set ranges from $2.0B (low) to $6.0B (high), reflecting heterogeneity in maturity and capital efficiency across the climate tech landscape.

Triangulating these two methods produces an implied range of $4.2B to $8.1B, with a median of $6.0B. The current $5.4B valuation sits within this range, near the peer median. Key uncertainties include the trajectory and timing of future financings—stage-progression multiples are highly sensitive to market conditions—and the appropriate peer set composition as Helion moves toward commercialization. The relatively wide range ($4.2B–$8.1B) reflects genuine uncertainty in stage-appropriate valuation for pre-revenue frontier energy technologies.
